> **Challenge window closes: April 24, 2026.** Challenges filed before this date will receive written substantive responses before activation proceeds. Challenges filed after are not part of the pre-activation record.

## What you are challenging

<!-- Select one or more: -->
- [ ] Part 1 — Circular dependency analysis (the bootstrap problem or a ruled-out alternative)
- [ ] Part 2 — Founding exception statement (scope, limits, or failure consequences)
- [ ] Part 3 — Proposed panel composition (a specific seat, conflict disclosure, or the adversarial member designation)
- [ ] Part 4 — The activation sequence itself (an AH clause or stage)
- [ ] Other — explain below

## The specific claim you are disputing

<!-- Quote the exact text from P014_Preactivation_Disclosure.md that you are challenging. Be precise. Vague objections will be asked to specify. -->

## Your reasoning

<!-- Why is the claim wrong, incomplete, or structurally dangerous? Logical analysis, evidence, or real-world analogue. -->

## Your proposed alternative

<!-- What would you do instead, and why is it better? Or: what question needs to be answered before this can proceed? If you are identifying a flaw without a solution, say so — that is still a valid challenge. -->

---

*Challenges to P-013's content (not P-014's process) belong in a standard Design Critique issue, not here. Challenges to the protocol's broader design belong in a standard Design Critique issue. This template is scoped to P-014 only.*
